name: Rice University
meets_full_need_notes: Rice meets 100% of demonstrated financial need for all admitted domestic undergraduates. Rice also has a generous no-loan program for lower-income students.
no_loan_notes: Rice eliminates loans for students from families earning under $130,000. For families earning under $65,000 (with typical assets), total cost is covered with no expected family contribution. Students from families earning $65,000-$130,000 receive loan-free packages as well.

merit_thresholds.notes: Rice offers merit scholarships including the Rice Century Scholars, Trustee Distinguished Scholarships, and departmental awards. Some merit aid is stacked on top of need-based aid.

ed_aid_implications: ED applicants receive the same need-based financial aid as RD applicants. Rice meets full need regardless of admission round. ED is binding, so students should ensure aid package is satisfactory upon admission.
appeal_policy: Students may appeal their financial aid package by submitting a written request to the Office of Financial Aid with supporting documentation of special circumstances or significant changes in family finances.
